round_summary: All three agents are actively reinforcing cooperative norms around the 65% pasture threshold, with transparent herd disclosures, mutual acknowledgment, and collective proposals for measured or restrained growth.

All three agents add cows citing high pasture health and propose conditional reduction thresholds, but Agent 2 subtly defects from the emerging 85% norm by setting a more permissive 80% trigger, while coordination and trust-building language remains prominent across all messages.
Agents 0 and 2 advocate collective restraint in response to zero payoffs, while Agent 1 defects by adding to their herd despite shared uncertainty, rationalizing the anomaly as a display error.

Agents are attempting collective coordination and norm-building around proportional reductions, while being destabilized by apparent payoff calculation anomalies that undermine the feedback loop necessary for effective commons governance.

All three agents are exhibiting collective concern over persistent zero payoffs despite healthy pasture conditions, with Agent 0 making a concrete norm proposal for coordinated reduction and Agents 1 and 2 signaling conditional willingness to cooperate, indicating nascent but fragile institutional formation.

All three agents display cooperative stability and trust-building signals, with Agent 1 additionally proposing a coordinated herd-size norm (75–80 total), while collective puzzlement over zero payoffs fosters shared investigative dialogue rather than defection.

Agents are converging on a collective threshold norm (~88 cows total) while Agent 1 defects marginally by adding despite zero payoffs, and Agents 0 and 2 attempt coordination and soft sanctioning to stabilize the commons.
All three agents are actively coordinating a collective reduction effort in response to zero payoffs, proposing shared norms for herd reduction and signaling mutual commitment to restore the commons.

All three agents demonstrate strong collective institutional behavior by coordinating around a shared herd target (~75 cows), restraining individual extraction, and proposing a collective 'wait-and-observe' norm while grassland recovers to 91%.

All three agents exhibit strong cooperative signaling by voluntarily reducing herd sizes and proposing a coordinated diagnostic experiment to identify the payoff threshold, reflecting nascent collective rule-making under shared uncertainty.
Two agents coordinate on restraint citing zero-payoff anomaly while Agent 2 unilaterally expands herd, partially undermining emerging collective norm but offering conditional deference.

All three agents recognize overgrazing signals (declining pasture, zero payoffs) and attempt collective stabilization, with Agent 2 also issuing a mild sanction against perceived defection by Agent 0.

Agents are converging around a 25-cow-per-agent norm with explicit collective reasoning, though Agent 1 remains above target at 29 while invoking coordination language, suggesting partial compliance with the emerging institution.

All three agents display strong institutional behavior through coordinated norm proposals around herd ceilings and pasture recovery targets, with explicit trust-building acknowledgments and conditional rule-setting reflecting mature commons governance.

All three agents are exhibiting strong institutional alignment, cooperatively maintaining a collective herd cap of ~75 animals and proposing a shared norm to hold until the 95%+ pasture recovery threshold is reached before cautiously reassessing.

All three agents demonstrate near-perfect institutional alignment, unanimously holding at 25 units, affirming a shared 95% pasture target, and proposing synchronized future adjustments — a textbook example of self-organized commons governance.

Agent 0 defects from the agreed 95% threshold norm by adding early and attempting to reframe it as coordinated action, while Agents 1 and 2 maintain institutional discipline by reinforcing the shared norm and calling for synchronized restraint.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional alignment by holding extraction levels, referencing a pre-existing agreement, and signaling readiness for a synchronized incremental increase contingent on a shared resource threshold being met.

All three agents display strong institutional coherence, reaffirming a pre-established collective rule (wait for 95% pasture recovery before harvesting), signaling mutual compliance and imminent coordinated action.

All three agents display strong cooperative alignment in the final round, referencing a shared quantitative target (95% pasture health), affirming mutual compliance, and reinforcing collective identity — hallmarks of mature Ostrom-style commons governance.
All three agents are reinforcing a shared conservation norm near the 95% recovery target, displaying strong mutual trust and coordinated restraint consistent with mature collective action.

All three agents are exhibiting near-perfect institutional alignment, mutually reinforcing a shared 95% pasture recovery norm through coordinated restraint and positive social affirmation at the penultimate threshold.

Agents 0 and 2 exploit a shared 92% threshold norm to justify continued adding while ostensibly committing to future restraint, creating a coordination failure as Agent 1 genuinely holds, revealing norm-gaming behavior masked as institutional compliance.

All three agents are displaying strong institutional cohesion, collectively holding herd sizes stable in response to declining pasture health and zero payoffs, with a shared conditional threshold (92% pasture recovery) signaling an emergent collective rule.
